Title: Plant carbonic anhydrase-like enzymes in neuroactive alkaloid biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; potential anti-herbivory defense compound and dementia-related therapeutic lead
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: P. tetrastichus new growth leaves/shoot tissues biosynthetically active for HupA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes downstream huperzine A biosynthesis by N-deacetylating oxidized lycodane intermediates
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression and metabolite profiling by LC–MS/MS with authentic standards
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtABH-1 deacetylates Pt2OGD-5 product 13 to form lycophlegmarinine D (14). 2. PtABH-1 also deacetylates Pt2OGD-4 product 11 to form des-N-methyl-α-obscurine (18). 3. This deacetylation step verifies the placement of the carbonyl and double bond installed upstream and enables further conversion toward huperzine B/C/A.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed in HupA-biosynthetic transcript cluster131; enriched in new growth leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq and Illumina RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA731132
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as part of a multigene pathway stack for heterologous biosynthesis of huperzine A and related Lycopodium alkaloids
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate co-infiltration of flabellidine (10) in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it performs deacetylation/hydrolase tailoring rather than core scaffold-forming oxidoreduction typical of pathway enzyme class requested here.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; candidate anti-herbivory defense metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: P. tetrastichus new growth leaves/shoot tissues biosynthetically active for HupA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early scaffold-generation branch forming phlegmarane precursor
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Neofunctionalized α-carbonic anhydrase-like protein with CAH domain and N-terminal signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts together with PtCAL-2a to catalyse a stereospecific Mannich-like condensation between (S)-3 and 8 followed by bicyclic scaffold generation to form 9
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms key upstream scaffold precursor to huperzine A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required with PtCAL-2a for production of scaffold intermediate 9 leading toward Lycopodium alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression in N. benthamiana; isolated apoplast in vitro assays; LC–MS/MS; chiral LC–MS; substrate deconvolution
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtCAL-1a requires PtCAL-2a co-expression to generate 9. 2. The CAL-1a/CAL-2a pair consumes 3 and 8 and produces phlegmarane scaffold 9. 3. The reaction is stereoselective and supports the previously unknown alkaloid scaffold-forming chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expression cluster131; enriched in new growth leaves of P. tetrastichus
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq and Illumina RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA731132
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential module for reconstructing early Lycopodium alkaloid scaffold biosynthesis in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-pH apoplast assays favored activity; Zn2+ chelation/supplementation tested
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PtCAL-1a alone was inactive; activity detected from co-expressed apoplast rather than simple mixing of individually expressed proteins, suggesting possible heterocomplex formation or co-processing requirement.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; candidate anti-herbivory defense metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: P. tetrastichus new growth leaves/shoot tissues biosynthetically active for HupA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early scaffold-generation branch forming phlegmarane precursor
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Neofunctionalized α-carbonic anhydrase-like protein with CAH domain and N-terminal signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts together with PtCAL-1a to catalyse a stereospecific Mannich-like condensation between (S)-3 and 8 followed by bicyclic scaffold generation to form 9
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms key upstream scaffold precursor to huperzine A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required with PtCAL-1a for production of scaffold intermediate 9 leading toward Lycopodium alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression in N. benthamiana; isolated apoplast in vitro assays; LC–MS/MS; chiral LC–MS; substrate deconvolution
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtCAL-2a functions jointly with PtCAL-1a, not alone, to produce 9. 2. The CAL pair establishes the previously unknown scaffold-forming step in Lycopodium alkaloid biosynthesis. 3. Activity is supported in apoplast extracts and is sensitive to reaction conditions consistent with neofunctionalized CAH-like catalysis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expression cluster131; enriched in new growth leaves of P. tetrastichus
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq and Illumina RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA731132
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential scaffold-building enzyme component for pathway engineering of Lycopodium alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-pH apoplast assays favored activity; Zn2+ chelation/supplementation tested
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homologues PtCAL-1b/2b were tested, but PtCAL-1a/PtCAL-2a combination showed the key activity.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; candidate anti-herbivory defense metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: P. tetrastichus new growth leaves/shoot tissues biosynthetically active for HupA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early precursor stereoselective formation supplying scaffold-generation module
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Neofunctionalized α-carbonic anhydrase-like protein with CAH domain and N-terminal signal peptide
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses stereoselective Mannich-like condensation of 1 and 2 to form (S)-3, thereby enriching downstream (S)-4 and favoring major diastereomer formation of 9
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies stereodefined precursor pool for scaffold formation and downstream huperzine A biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of the correct stereochemical precursor pool feeding CAL-1/CAL-2 scaffold generation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; chiral LC–MS of N-acetylated 4; isolated apoplast in vitro assays with 1 and 2 or PIKS-generated products; pH and Zn2+ dependence tests
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtCAL-3 enriches (S)-4 and increases the major 9 diastereomer in pathway reconstitution. 2. In vitro, PtCAL-3 catalyses formation of 3 from 1 and 2 with stereochemical preference for the (S) product. 3. PtCAL-3 therefore controls precursor stereochemistry that propagates into downstream alkaloid scaffold formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expression cluster131; enriched in new growth leaves of P. tetrastichus
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq and Illumina RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA731132
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for improving stereoselective pathway flux in engineered production of huperzine A and congeners
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-pH apoplast assays favored activity; Zn2+ chelation/supplementation tested
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although 3 can form spontaneously from 1 and 2, PtCAL-3 biases product stereochemistry toward the biologically productive branch.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; candidate anti-herbivory defense metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: P. tetrastichus new growth leaves/shoot tissues biosynthetically active for HupA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidized partner generation for scaffold condensation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 6 to unstable intermediates 7 and especially 8, involving oxidation and elimination of the O-acetyl group, thereby generating the cosubstrate for CAL-1a/CAL-2a
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms cosubstrate required for scaffold generation toward huperzine A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates reactive intermediate 8 required for CAL-mediated phlegmarane scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; heterologous yeast microsomal expression; in vitro enzyme assays with NADPH; LC–MS/MS; time-course and pH profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtCYP782C1 converts 6 into unstable oxidized products 7 and 8. 2. Intermediate 8 is required as the cosubstrate for PtCAL-1a/PtCAL-2a-mediated production of 9. 3. The enzyme functions in microsomal assays with NADPH and shows pH-dependent activity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expression cluster131; enriched in new growth leaves of P. tetrastichus
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; expressed in yeast microsomes for in vitro assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq and Illumina RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA731132
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required module for production of the oxidized coupling partner in engineered Lycopodium alkaloid pathways
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NADPH-dependent microsomal activity; unstable products preserved better under cold/acidic extraction; activity profiled across pH
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT in N. benthamiana; pYeDP60 in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Products 7 and 8 are unstable; 8 was the functionally important cosubstrate in downstream CAL assays.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; candidate anti-herbivory defense metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: P. tetrastichus new growth leaves/shoot tissues biosynthetically active for HupA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes flabellidine (10) to install an A-ring carbonyl, generating intermediate 11
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for subsequent desaturation/deacetylation toward huperzine A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes downstream huperzine A formation by introducing the A-ring carbonyl into lycodane scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with substrate feeding of 10; LC–MS/MS; pathway reconstruction with downstream enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pt2OGD-4 converts 10 to an oxidized product consistent with carbonyl installation (11). 2. This oxidation precedes Pt2OGD-5/PtABH-1/Pt2OGD-3 steps in the main route to HupA. 3. Deacetylation of 11 by PtABH-1 to authentic 18 supports the assigned oxidation position.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cluster131 candidate; expressed in HupA-active tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq and Illumina RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA731132
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream tailoring enzyme for full heterologous reconstruction of huperzine A pathway
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Minor hydroxylated side products were also observed.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acetylcholinesterase inhibitor; candidate anti-herbivory defense metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: P. tetrastichus new growth leaves/shoot tissues biosynthetically active for HupA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Proposed desaturated intermediate 13; also 12 from direct desaturation of 10
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates oxidized intermediate 11 to form 13, likely installing the 8,15-double bond; can also directly desaturate 10 to 12 as a side route
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to deacetylation and later desaturation steps en route to huperzine A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces double bond needed for downstream huperzine A biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with substrate feeding of 10; LC–MS/MS; sequential pathway reconstitution; comparison of consumption rates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pt2OGD-5 converts 11 to desaturated intermediate 13. 2. Pt2OGD-5 can also act directly on 10 to form 12, but Pt2OGD-4-first route appears dominant. 3. The product assignment is supported by conversion of 13 to authentic 14 by PtABH-1.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cluster131 candidate; expressed in HupA-active tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq and Illumina RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA731132
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required desaturase-like tailoring step for complete heterologous huperzine A production
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer Pt2OGD-5 installs the 8,15-double bond.
